visa_types.name: Tourist Visa
visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Please note: The consul may request that you re-take your fingerprints if they are not recorded by the system, in which case you will need to return to the Visa Application Centre.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
1.Minors (under 18 years of age) who are not accompanied to travel to Italy by one or both parents. In such instances, the parent(s) are required to personally come and sign the 'atto di assenso' (act of consent) directly at the IVAC.
For parents residing abroad, this 'atto di assenso' must be signed and subsequently legalized by the Italian Embassy or Consulate within their country of residence. Conversely, if the parent(s) are located in Italy, legalization of the 'atto di assenso' can be procured either from the Comune or the Embassy of Indonesia in Rome.
In exceptional circumstances, particularly for parents whose professional duties, such as military or police service, preclude their presence in Jakarta, a localized notarization of the 'atto di assenso' may be permissible. This notarization must explicitly state that 'the document was signed in front of the notary.' Such cases will be considered on a strictly individual basis and necessitate prior approval from the Embassy.
It is crucial to note that court orders (custody letter) are no longer deemed acceptable as a substitute or exemption for the 'atto di assenso,' even in cases where parents are divorced; They must anyway come in person (Their presence remains mandatory).
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
Tourist visa
- Please fill out the application e-form available at this link: https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html).
                                                                            Once completed, print and sign the form.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
- Proof of financial means.
- Bank reference.
- Certified copy of your recent personal bank statement (from the last three months) stamped or legalized by the bank.
Please note that even if your travel expenses are covered by others, you still need to provide proof of your own financial means.
- Proof of socio-economic status.
- An employment letter that includes the employer's contact information, specifies the applicant's position and duration of employment, and authorizes leave for the travel period to the Schengen area. It should also guarantee that the employment relationship will continue upon return.
- This letter must be accompanied by a photocopy of the company's registration from the local Chamber of Commerce (e.g., SIUP, NIB, or TDP).
- Sponsorship letter if the applicant is not currently employed.
- For students, an enrollment letter from the school or university is needed.
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Tourist visit:
- Proof of accommodation for the entire stay: For organized trips, provide a complete travel itinerary from the travel agency. For self-organized trips, provide both the travel itinerary and prepaid accommodation reservations.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application
On April 5, 2010, regulation no. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) enters into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.
visa_types.visa_fees:
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[ita > idn > en > Table:Tourist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 321.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee. VFS service fee is paid online when scheduling an appointment.
The VFS service fee and all value-added services (VAS) charges include VAT.
The visa fee and other charges can be paid via QR code, debit or credit card, or in cash in Indonesian Rupiah.
An official receipt must be obtained for all payments made (including changes to the IDR VFS service fee). No additional fees will be requested for visa issuance or for expedited processing of the visa.
Terms  and Conditions:
Please note that logistics fees and other additional service fees are non-refundable once the transaction has been made.
visa_types.processing_time:
Applications accepted at the Italy Visa Application Centre will be submitted on the next working day to the Embassy of Italy for a decision. Visa processing time will take maximum 15
                                                                        calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta.

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for National visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. 
Please note that with the approval of Legislative Decree 11 October 2024, n. 145, starting from 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to acquire fingerprints also for requests regarding the issuance of national entry visas (type D).
The methods of acquisition and any exceptions are the same as those currently in force for short-stay visas (art. 13 of the Visa Code). Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
- Both parents must come in person to the Visa Application Centre to complete and sign the consent letter, which will be provided at the time of visa application submission. (Required)
- In the case of a divorce, if the court ruling specifies which parent has been given custody, the custodial parent will not require consent from the other parent.
They however will still need to come in person to the Italian Visa Application Centre to sign the application form and consent letter (as outlined in point 1), if the ruling does not explicitly state custody arrangements.
Note:  - The parents must sign the visa application form (for number 1).
The court sentence can be provided in copy (for number 2). 
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Application form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/formulario-visto-nazionaleing-ita-nuovo-2020.pdf) duly completed and signed by the applicant.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid flight reservation (a one-way ticket is acceptable).
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Family Visit for Re-entry:
A letter specifying the reason for requesting the re-entry visa, along with the following attachments:
- A copy of the expired permesso di soggiorno
- A copy of the renewal kit receipt from the Ufficio Postale in Italy
- A copy of the convocation notices from the Questura for fingerprinting or for collecting the permesso di soggiorno (if applicable)
On April 5, 2010, Regulation No. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and Council, which establishes a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code), came into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

- Full itinerary.
- Date and airport of entry into the territory of the Member State.
- Port of embarkation.
- Duration of the seafarer’s stay on the ship.
- Port of disembarkation, if applicable.
- Date of return to Indonesia, if applicable.
- The maritime agency should also provide the name and address of the Indonesian agency it collaborates with, which will handle the visa application submission or be responsible for transporting the seafarer to the seaport upon arrival in the Member State.
- Copy of the duly signed Employment Contract.

- Seaman’s book valid for the entire duration of the assignment.
- A copy of the seaman’s book.
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).

If the visa application is submitted by an Indonesian maritime agency, the following documents are required:
- Letter from the Indonesian maritime agency confirming the seafarer(s)' employment on the ship.

- Personal data: Seafarer's full name, place and date of birth, passport number (issue date and expiry date).
- The seafarer’s position on the ship (if there are multiple seafarers, this information may be included in an attached signed and stamped list).
- Name of the ship.
- Full itinerary.
- Date and airport of entry into the territory of the Member State.
- Port of embarkation.
- Duration of the seafarer’s stay on the ship.
- Port of disembarkation, if applicable.
- Date of return to Indonesia, if applicable.
- The maritime agency should also provide the name and address of the Indonesian agency it collaborates with, which will handle the visa application submission or be responsible for transporting the seafarer to the seaport upon arrival in the Member State.

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Please note: The consul may request that you re-take your fingerprints if they are not recorded by the system, in which case you will need to return to the Visa Application Centre.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
1.  Minors (under 18 years of age) who are not accompanied to travel to Italy by one or both parents. In such instances, the parent(s) are required to personally come and sign the 'atto di assenso' (act of consent) directly at the IVAC.
 For parents residing abroad, this 'atto di assenso' must be signed and subsequently legalized by the Italian Embassy or Consulate within their country of residence. Conversely, if the parent(s) are located in Italy, legalization of the 'atto di assenso' can be procured either from the Comune or the Embassy of Indonesia in Rome.
In exceptional circumstances, particularly for parents whose professional duties, such as military or police service, preclude their presence in Jakarta, a localized notarization of the 'atto di assenso' may be permissible. This notarization must explicitly state that 'the document was signed in front of the notary.' Such cases will be considered on a strictly individual basis and necessitate prior approval from the Embassy.
It is crucial to note that court orders (custody letter) are no longer deemed acceptable as a substitute or exemption for the 'atto di assenso,' even in cases where parents are divorced; They must anyway come in person (Their presence remains mandatory).
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Please fill out the application e-form available at this link: https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html).
                                                                            Once completed, print and sign the form.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
- Proof of financial means.
- Bank reference.
- Certified copy of your recent personal bank statement (from the last three months) stamped or legalized by the bank.
Please note that even if your travel expenses are covered by others, you still need to provide proof of your own financial means.
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Study:
- A certificate of enrollment or a registration letter from the Italian school, university, or any other officially recognized institution. This document should specify the type of course, its duration, the start date, and the full address of the institution. Note: The document must confirm that the course includes at least 20 hours of class per week.
- Proof of financial support for a study visa is required at a rate of €453 per month (equivalent to €5,889 per year). This can be demonstrated through:
- Proof of personal or family financial resources, or

Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Please note: The consul may request that you re-take your fingerprints if they are not recorded by the system, in which case you will need to return to the Visa Application Centre.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
1.  Minors (under 18 years of age) who are not accompanied to travel to Italy by one or both parents. In such instances, the parent(s) are required to personally come and sign the 'atto di assenso' (act of consent) directly at the IVAC.
 For parents residing abroad, this 'atto di assenso' must be signed and subsequently legalized by the Italian Embassy or Consulate within their country of residence. Conversely, if the parent(s) are located in Italy, legalization of the 'atto di assenso' can be procured either from the Comune or the Embassy of Indonesia in Rome.
In exceptional circumstances, particularly for parents whose professional duties, such as military or police service, preclude their presence in Jakarta, a localized notarization of the 'atto di assenso' may be permissible. This notarization must explicitly state that 'the document was signed in front of the notary.' Such cases will be considered on a strictly individual basis and necessitate prior approval from the Embassy.
It is crucial to note that court orders (custody letter) are no longer deemed acceptable as a substitute or exemption for the 'atto di assenso,' even in cases where parents are divorced; They must anyway come in person (Their presence remains mandatory).
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.

(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Please fill out the application e-form available at this link: https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html).
                                                                            Once completed, print and sign the form.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
- Proof of financial means.
- Bank reference.
- Certified copy of your recent personal bank statement (from the last three months) stamped or legalized by the bank.
Please note that even if your travel expenses are covered by others, you still need to provide proof of your own financial means.
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Study:
- A certificate of enrollment or a registration letter from the Italian school, university, or any other officially recognized institution. This document should specify the type of course, its duration, the start date, and the full address of the institution. Note: The document must confirm that the course includes at least 20 hours of class per week.
- Proof of financial support for a study visa is required at a rate of €453 per month (equivalent to €5,889 per year). This can be demonstrated through:
- Proof of personal or family financial resources, or
- A financial guarantee from accredited Italian institutions or agencies, local governments, or foreign institutions or agencies deemed reliable by this Embassy.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application
On April 5, 2010, regulation no. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) enters into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

## Processing Time
Applications accepted at the Italy Visa Application Centre will be submitted on the next working day to the Embassy of Italy for a decision. Visa processing time will take maximum 15
                                                                        calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual. 
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta.

visa_types.overview:
How to apply?
Effective 21st June 2024, applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the customer, to the
                                                            VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, Monday to Friday from 8.00am to 3.00pm. The address of the VFS Italy Visa Application Center is as follows: 
Call Center  : +62 21 30058877
Email:info.italyid@vfshelpline.com (mailto:info.italyid@vfshelpline.com)
Italy Visa Application Center (IVAC)
Chubb Square Thamrin Nine, 9th floor 
Jl. MH Thamrin no. 8-9, Jakarta
The application form, the invitation format, the visa fee and the list of the documentation required for every type of visa, can be found on this website.
For general information on visa regulations, categories and requirements please visit www.ambjakarta.esteri.it (http://www.ambjakarta.esteri.it/) or Ministry of Foreign Affair’s website (https://www.esteri.it/en/). 
PLEASE NOTE:
The Embassy of Italy has the right to request further documentation and that by submitting the required documentation does not necessarily imply that the visa will be granted.
While collection of the application will be managed by the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, any assessment and decision on visa applications are taken by the Embassy only. 
Identify Your Visa Type.
When scheduling your appointment online, ensure you select the correct visa type based on the purpose of your visit. For detailed information about visa types related to your appointment, please carefully review the details provided below.
C- SHORT STAY VISA CATEGORIES – SCHENGEN VISA
- Tourist Visa
- Vacation, Sightseeing.
- Conference, Exhibition, Concert/Festival.
- Visit Non-EU Family.
- Visit Friends.
- Business Visa
- Business meetings, trainings, FAT (Factory Acceptance Test), or other visit with commercial nature (with providing the invitation letter (Affari) and the copy of the first 4 (four) pages of registration to the relevant Italian Chamber of Commerce (Visura Camerale).
- Study Visa
- Short Courses.
- Seaman Transit Visa
- This is a short-stay visa allowing entry to the Schengen area for the sole purpose of transiting through Italy to reach another country.
- Others
- Official Invitation from Italian Government or CONI (Scientific, Cultural, Sport, Politic).
- Short visit for Official Mission (holding diplomatic, service and other passport).
- Subordinate Work Visa (working visa: provide original authorization letter “Nulla Osta” issued by “Spotello Unico per l’immigrazione”).
- Religious Purpose
- EU Spouse/Family (Family reunification)
When to apply?
A visa application should be lodged at least 15 calendar days before the intended visit and cannot be lodged earlier than six months before the start of the intended visit. Visa processing time
                                                            will take maximum 15 calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual. That period may be extended up to a maximum of 30 calendar days in individual cases,
                                                            notably when further scrutiny of the application is needed. Exceptionally, when additional documentation is needed in specific cases, the period may be extended up to a maximum of 60 calendar
                                                            days.
                                                        
 An application lodged less than 5 calendar days before the intended departure may be accepted, but the processing time may take more than 5 days and the decision might be taken AFTER the intended
                                                            date of departure. It is the customer’s responsibility to take necessary precautions to respect the deadlines where an appointment system is in place. A Holder of a multiple-entry visa valid
                                                            for a period of at least six month may lodge the application for a new visa before the expiry of the validity of the visa currently held. However, the validity of the new visa must complement
                                                            the current visa, a person cannot hold two uniform visas valid for the same period of time.
Before applying, please ensure you are very clear on your ‘purpose of visit’ – do remember we are here to assist and help you through the entire visa application process but are not permitted
                                                            to advise or guide you on choosing a visa category. Since our work is primarily administrative in nature, we have no say on whether you will be granted a visa and how long it will take to process
                                                            your application, as this is entirely the prerogative of the Embassy of Italy.
Please ensure you read the Security  Regulations notices before you visit the Visa Application Centre.

## Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for study.
Click on the link Do you need a visa? (https://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/?lang=en_US)

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows;

[ita > idn > en > Table:Study > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 321.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee. VFS service fee is paid online when scheduling an appointment.
The VFS service fee and all value-added services (VAS) charges include VAT. 
The visa fee and other charges can be paid via QR code, debit or credit card, or in cash in Indonesian Rupiah.
An official receipt must be obtained for all payments made (including changes to the IDR VFS service fee). No additional fees will be requested for visa issuance or for expedited processing of the visa.
Terms  and Conditions:
Please note that logistics fees and other additional service fees are non-refundable once the transaction has been made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for National visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. 
Please note that with the approval of Legislative Decree 11 October 2024, n. 145, starting from 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to acquire fingerprints also for requests regarding the issuance of national entry visas (type D).
The methods of acquisition and any exceptions are the same as those currently in force for short-stay visas (art. 13 of the Visa Code). Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
- Both parents must come in person to the Visa Application Centre to complete and sign the consent letter, which will be provided at the time of visa application submission. (Required)
- In the case of a divorce, if the court ruling specifies which parent has been given custody, the custodial parent will not require consent from the other parent.
They however will still need to come in person to the Italian Visa Application Centre to sign the application form and consent letter (as outlined in point 1), if the ruling does not explicitly state custody arrangements.
Note:  - The parents must sign the visa application form (for number 1).
- The court sentence can be provided in copy (for number 2). 
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Application form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/formulario-visto-nazionaleing-ita-nuovo-2020.pdf) duly completed and signed by the applicant.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Study:
- Proof of accommodation in Italy: declaration of hospitality or hotel reservation (If required)
- A certificate of enrolment or a registration letter from the Italian school, university, or any other officially recognized institution. This document should specify the type of course, its duration, the start date, and the full address of the institution. Note: The document must confirm that the course includes at least 20 hours of class per week.
- Proof of financial support for a study visa is required at a rate of €453 per month (equivalent to €5,889 per year). This can be demonstrated through:
- Proof of personal or family financial resources, or
- A financial guarantee from accredited Italian institutions or agencies, local governments, or foreign institutions or agencies deemed reliable by this Embassy.
- Documentation of the Pre-registration (Preinscrizione) trough Uni-Italia Indoneisa.
Attention: The consul may request additional documents beyond those already submitted with the application.
On April 5, 2010, Regulation No. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and Council, which establishes a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code), came into force.
These new regulations introduce additional requirements for the application process, stipulating that all supporting documents must be submitted along with the National application form.
If supporting documents are not provided at the time of application, your application may be refused upon examination, and the visa fee may be forfeited.

## Photo Specifications
For Photo Specifications, please click here (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1YHvqJA2ST8BKXxDHJLxE7/b632819c3156c9b9e7776ec407e61c87/icao-photo.pdf);

## Processing Time
Applications accepted at the Italy Visa Application Centre will be submitted on the next working day to the Embassy of Italy for a decision. Visa processing time will take maximum 15
                                                                        calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual. 
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy  of Italy in Jakarta.

(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Please fill out the application e-form available at this link: https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html).
                                                                            Once completed, print and sign the form.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).

- Copy of the marriage certificate issued by the town hall of a European Union country, or a copy of the marriage acknowledgment/registration from the respective embassy in Jakarta. Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application
On April 5, 2010, regulation no. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) enters into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

                                                                        calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual.

Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Please note: The consul may request that you re-take your fingerprints if they are not recorded by the system, in which case you will need to return to the Visa Application Centre.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
1.Minors (under 18 years of age) who are not accompanied to travel to Italy by one or both parents. In such instances, the parent(s) are required to personally come and sign the 'atto di assenso' (act of consent) directly at the IVAC.
For parents residing abroad, this 'atto di assenso' must be signed and subsequently legalized by the Italian Embassy or Consulate within their country of residence. Conversely, if the parent(s) are located in Italy, legalization of the 'atto di assenso' can be procured either from the Comune or the Embassy of Indonesia in Rome.
In exceptional circumstances, particularly for parents whose professional duties, such as military or police service, preclude their presence in Jakarta, a localized notarization of the 'atto di assenso' may be permissible. This notarization must explicitly state that 'the document was signed in front of the notary.' Such cases will be considered on a strictly individual basis and necessitate prior approval from the Embassy.
It is crucial to note that court orders (custody letter) are no longer deemed acceptable as a substitute or exemption for the 'atto di assenso,' even in cases where parents are divorced; They must anyway come in person (Their presence remains mandatory).
With the issuance of DPCM 07 September 2020, travellers from Indonesia can now enter Italy if they have a proven and stable affective relationship (regardless of cohabitation) with Italian, EU, or Schengen citizens, or with individuals who are legally residing in Italy on a long-term basis, provided they are traveling to their partner’s home, domicile, or dwelling in Italy. This will be classified as a tourist visa for a family, or friends visit. One of the key requirements is that the partner in Italy must be resident and present in the country.

- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
- Proof of financial means.
- Bank reference.
- Certified copy of your recent personal bank statement (from the last three months) stamped or legalized by the bank.
Please note that even if your travel expenses are covered by others, you still need to provide proof of your own financial means.
- Proof of socio-economic status.
- An employment letter that includes the employer's contact information, specifies the applicant's position and duration of employment, and authorizes leave for the travel period to the Schengen area. It should also guarantee that the employment relationship will continue upon return.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Application is applicable in case you are travelling for political, scientific or cultural reason, sports events or religious purposes.
Click on the link Do you need a visa? (https://vistoperitalia.esteri.it/?lang=en_US)

## Visa Fees
The Visa Fees are as follows:

[ita > idn > en > Table:Official Invitation (Scientific, Cultural, Sport) > Visa Fees 1]
Note:
A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 321.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee. VFS service fee is paid online when scheduling an appointment.
The VFS service fee and all value-added services (VAS) charges include VAT. 
The visa fee and other charges can be paid via QR code, debit or credit card, or in cash in Indonesian Rupiah.
An official receipt must be obtained for all payments made (including changes to the IDR VFS service fee). No additional fees will be requested for visa issuance or for expedited processing of the visa.
Terms  and Conditions:
Please note that logistics fees and other additional service fees are non-refundable once the transaction has been made.

## Documents Required
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for Schengen Visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Please note: The consul may request that you re-take your fingerprints if they are not recorded by the system, in which case you will need to return to the Visa Application Centre.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
1. Minors (under 18 years of age) who are not accompanied to travel to Italy by one or both parents. In such instances, the parent(s) are required to personally come and sign the 'atto di assenso' (act of consent) directly at the IVAC.
For parents residing abroad, this 'atto di assenso' must be signed and subsequently legalized by the Italian Embassy or Consulate within their country of residence. Conversely, if the parent(s) are located in Italy, legalization of the 'atto di assenso' can be procured either from the Comune or the Embassy of Indonesia in Rome.
In exceptional circumstances, particularly for parents whose professional duties, such as military or police service, preclude their presence in Jakarta, a localized notarization of the 'atto di assenso' may be permissible. This notarization must explicitly state that 'the document was signed in front of the notary.' Such cases will be considered on a strictly individual basis and necessitate prior approval from the Embassy.
It is crucial to note that court orders (custody letter) are no longer deemed acceptable as a substitute or exemption for the 'atto di assenso,' even in cases where parents are divorced; They must anyway come in person (Their presence remains mandatory).
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Please fill out the application e-form available at this link:
https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html)
.
                                                                            Once completed, print and sign the form.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
- Travel medical insurance for the entire period of stay, which must include:
- Name of the insured person and the insurance validity period.
- Minimum coverage of EUR 30,000.
- Coverage for the entire Schengen area, not just Italy.
- Coverage for the full duration of your stay in the Schengen area, as indicated by your flight details.
Please be aware that insurance coverage may be checked at the Schengen border. Insufficient proof of insurance could result in refusal of entry. For references to insurance companies, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/List-Schengen-insurance-companies.pdf).
- Proof of financial means.
- Bank reference.
- Certified copy of your recent personal bank statement (from the last three months) stamped or legalized by the bank.
Please note that even if your travel expenses are covered by others, you still need to provide proof of your own financial means.
- Proof of socio-economic status.
- An employment letter that includes the employer's contact information, specifies the applicant's position and duration of employment, and authorizes leave for the travel period to the Schengen area. It should also guarantee that the employment relationship will continue upon return.
- This letter must be accompanied by a photocopy of the company's registration from the local Chamber of Commerce (e.g., SIUP, NIB, or TDP).
- Sponsorship letter if the applicant is not currently employed.
- For students, an enrollment letter from the school or university is needed.
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Scientific, Cultural, or Sporting Events:
Trips for Scientific or Cultural Purposes:
- A letter of invitation from the inviting entity in the Member State of destination. The letter should include the name of the organization, contact information, details of the invited person, dates of stay in the Schengen area, and the reasons for the invitation.
To Attend Sporting Events:
- A letter of invitation from the sports federation in Italy, bearing the stamp of
CONI
.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application
On April 5, 2010, regulation no. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) enters into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

## Photo Specifications
For Photo Specifications, please click here;  (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/1YHvqJA2ST8BKXxDHJLxE7/b632819c3156c9b9e7776ec407e61c87/icao-photo.pdf)

## Processing Time
Applications accepted at the Italy Visa Application Centre will be submitted on the next working day to the Embassy of Italy for a decision. Visa processing time will take maximum 15
                                                                        calendar days, however during certain high season period it might take longer than usual.
All visa decisions are solely at the discretion of the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta.

## Download Forms
 E-application Schengen form (https://e-applicationvisa.esteri.it/index.html)

Schengen Manual form (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/4lfc8a94STd5lxzQK3Ly2y/e9c197362fdaee8eed7c367149fe81c6/new-schengen-visa-form.pdf)

Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)

Please note that with the approval of Legislative Decree 11 October 2024, n. 145, starting from 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to acquire fingerprints also for requests regarding the issuance of national entry visas (type D).

The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Application form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/formulario-visto-nazionaleing-ita-nuovo-2020.pdf) duly completed and signed by the applicant.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Seaman’s book valid for the entire duration of the assignment.
- A copy of the seaman’s book.
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid return flight reservation.
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Seaman long-term:
- Employment/sponsor letter (in English) from the local maritime agency, detailing the list of seamen and their employment status aboard the ship.
- Invitation letter from the maritime agency in Italy, indicating where the ship crew will board, duly endorsed/legalized by the Port Authority in Italy (Capitaneria di Porto).
- Certificate of the ship’s nationality and documents related to the ship’s registration in the International Register.
- Copy of the signed employment contract.
- Contract between the local agency and the Italian ship owner.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application

If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

visa_types.documents_required:

Applications for National visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00.

- Both parents must come in person to the Visa Application Centre to complete and sign the consent letter, which will be provided at the time of visa application submission. (Required)
- In the case of a divorce, if the court ruling specifies which parent has been given custody, the custodial parent will not require consent from the other parent.
They however will still need to come in person to the Italian Visa Application Centre to sign the application form and consent letter (as outlined in point 1), if the ruling does not explicitly state custody arrangements.
Note:  - The parents must sign the visa application form (for number 1).
- The court sentence can be provided in copy (for number 2). 
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.

(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)

- Must include the passport holder's signature.

Necessary supporting documents: 
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid flight reservation (a one-way ticket is acceptable).
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Family Visit for EU Citizen Member:
- Request letter from the EU citizen’s spouse, along with a copy of the EU citizen’s passport.
- Proof of Ties: For Children/Parents/Parents-in-law of an EU Citizen: Provide a copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and the birth certificate.
For EU Spouse: Provide a copy of the marriage certificate issued by the town hall in an EU country or a copy of the marriage acknowledgment/registration from the respective embassy in Jakarta. If the marriage is not registered in an EU country or at the respective embassy, you will need to submit the documents as you would for a regular tourist visa.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application

These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.

visa_types.documents_required:
Travel insurance is mandatory for all Schengen countries. If you have not purchased, please click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/travel-insurance)
Applications for National visas to the Embassy of Italy in Jakarta can be submitted, by scheduling an appointment and solely by personal appearance of the applicant, to the VFS Italy Visa Application Centre, only on Monday to Friday, from 08:00 to 15:00. 
Please note that with the approval of Legislative Decree 11 October 2024, n. 145, starting from 11 January 2025, it will be mandatory to acquire fingerprints also for requests regarding the issuance of national entry visas (type D).
The methods of acquisition and any exceptions are the same as those currently in force for short-stay visas (art. 13 of the Visa Code). Applicants who have rendered their fingerprint data in the last 59 months are NOT required to visit the Italy Visa Application Centre for fingerprint collection.
Eligible applicants may choose to send their documents to the VFS Visa Centre using either VFS’s courier service (Click here (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/idn/en/ita/premium-services) for available services) or their own courier provider. Please note that if you opt to use your own courier service, VFS will not be responsible for any misplaced documents, and you will need to coordinate directly with your chosen courier provider. * Terms and conditions apply.
Applicants under 18 years old (minors) must be accompanied by both parents (father and mother) when submitting their visa application. Please follow the procedure outlined below for minor visa applications:
- Both parents must come in person to the Visa Application Centre to complete and sign the consent letter, which will be provided at the time of visa application submission. (Required)
- In the case of a divorce, if the court ruling specifies which parent has been given custody, the custodial parent will not require consent from the other parent.
They however will still need to come in person to the Italian Visa Application Centre to sign the application form and consent letter (as outlined in point 1), if the ruling does not explicitly state custody arrangements.
Note:  - The parents must sign the visa application form (for number 1).
- The court sentence can be provided in copy (for number 2). 
Applicants can submit their visa application if Italy is the main destination.
The visa application should satisfy the following conditions:
(Please ensure that all documents are submitted in A4 size)
- Application form (https://visa.vfsglobal.com/one-pager/italy/indonesia/english/pdf/formulario-visto-nazionaleing-ita-nuovo-2020.pdf) duly completed and signed by the applicant.
- The applicant holds a valid travel document satisfying the following criteria:
- Contains at least two blank pages facing each other.
- Should be issued within the last 10 years.
- Must be valid for at least three months beyond the intended date of departure from the Schengen area.
- Must include the passport holder's signature.
- Your passport must display accurate personal information, with no corrections or additional items allowed on pages 4 or 5. If there are any errors in your identification data, you will need to obtain a new passport with the correct information.
- A photocopy of the pages containing the biometric data of the passport.
- Two recent color photos (35mm x 45mm) with a white or light-colored background, adhering to ICAO standards.
Necessary supporting documents: 
- Copy of the Family Status Certificate (Kartu Keluarga) and marriage or birth certificate (if traveling with the family).
- For foreign nationals: Copy of a valid residence permit (KITAS/KITAP).
- A valid flight reservation (a one-way ticket is acceptable).
Documents Establishing the Purpose of Family Visit for Non-EU Citizens:
- Copy, if applicable, of the authorization letter (NULLA OSTA) issued by the “Sportello Unico per l’Immigrazione” from the competent Prefettura in Italy.
Attention: The consul may request the presentation of further documents in addition to the ones already attached to the application
On April 5, 2010, regulation no. 810/2009 of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a Community Code on Visas (Visa Code) enters into force.
These new regulations imply new demands on the application, meaning that all supporting documents must be handed in along with the Schengen application form.
If supporting documents are not presented at the same time as the application, your application upon examination may be refused and the visa fee lost.

A service charge of Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) 321.000 is levied per applicant by VFS over and above the Visa Fee. VFS service fee is paid online when scheduling an appointment.
The VFS service fee and all value-added services (VAS) charges include VAT. 
The visa fee and other charges can be paid via QR code, debit or credit card, or in cash in Indonesian Rupiah.
An official receipt must be obtained for all payments made (including changes to the IDR VFS service fee). No additional fees will be requested for visa issuance or for expedited processing of the visa.

Please note that logistics fees and other additional service fees are non-refundable once the transaction has been made.
